title: Narcissus redoutei
taxonomy: Narcissus redoutei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Narcissus, which is a group of bulbous perennials commonly known as daffodils. The species is part of the family Amaryllidaceae and is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other Narcissus species and its unique characteristics. Narcissus redoutei is often placed in the section Pseudonarcissus, which includes several other species of daffodils with similar features. The classification of Narcissus redoutei has been refined over time through advances in botanical research and the development of new classification systems.

intro: Narcissus redoutei, also known as the Redoute's daffodil, is a species of flowering plant within the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its vibrant trumpet-shaped flowers and narrow, strap-like leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions, such as respiratory infections, wounds, and even cancer. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus redoutei include alkaloids like galantamine, lycorine, and homochlorcyclinine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea form, and it has also been used in the production of homeopathic remedies and medicinal extracts.
